description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the debian:bookworm-slim Docker image with a before_script that runs apt-get update && apt-get -y install git openjdk-17-jdk-headless. Define two stages: git-robot and build. Add a git-merge-upstream job in the git-robot stage that runs only on schedules and the master branch (excluding pushes) and executes the commands: git config --global user.name "Git Robot", git config --global user.email "git-robot@opentransitmap.org", eval $(ssh-agent -s), ssh-add <(echo "$SSH_PRIVATE_KEY"), mkdir -p ~/.ssh, create an SSH config disabling strict host key checking when inside Docker, git clone git@gitlab.com:${CI_PROJECT_PATH}.git, cd ${CI_PROJECT_NAME}, git remote add upstream https://github.com/schildbach/public-transport-enabler.git, git fetch upstream, git config merge.ours.driver true, git merge upstream/master, ./gradlew clean build, and git push --force origin master. Add a gradle-build job in the build stage that runs on the master branch and pushes (excluding schedules) and runs ./gradlew clean build.
